review_summary: Textual grounding is sufficient for concept-focused questions, and no separate video clip is needed.

qa_outputs.qa_rationale: The item is aligned, grounded, and does not need repair after the prior cleanup pass.
The item is aligned, grounded, and does not need repair after the prior cleanup pass.

repaired_question_bank.question: Why is mapping every unseen word to a single UNK token a weak strategy for modern NLP models?
choices: It erases information that may be recoverable from the word's characters or morphemes.
It makes common words too frequent during training.
It prevents the model from using punctuation as tokens.
It forces the model to learn a separate vector for every sentence.
repaired_question_bank.explanation: A novel word often contains meaningful parts, such as a stem, affix, typo pattern, or morphology. Collapsing all unseen strings into one UNK token makes very different words indistinguishable.

repaired_question_bank.question: In learned subword tokenization, what is the main purpose of merging frequent adjacent character sequences?
choices: To build larger reusable units so common words stay compact while rare words remain decomposable.
To remove all punctuation before the model sees the text.
To guarantee every word becomes exactly one token.
To replace all morphology with a language-specific grammar table.
repaired_question_bank.explanation: Starting from characters gives coverage for almost any string. Merging frequent pairs creates useful larger units, reducing sequence length for common patterns while preserving the ability to represent unseen forms.

item_calibration_bootstrap.calibration_rationale: "Why is mapping every unseen word to a single UNK token a weak strategy for modern NLP models" is a direct reinforcement check and should be on the easier side.
"In learned subword tokenization, what is the main purpose of merging frequent adjacent character sequences" asks for applied understanding rather than pure recall, so a medium prior fits.

item_phase_map.phase_rationale: "Why is mapping every unseen word to a single UNK token a weak strategy for modern NLP models" works best as an early concept check.
"In learned subword tokenization, what is the main purpose of merging frequent adjacent character sequences" is better after the learner already has the basic vocabulary.
